arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي
transliteration: Rabbish-rah li sadri wa yassir li amri
translation: My Lord, expand for me my chest and ease for me my task.

insights.meaning: The dua of Prophet Musa (AS) before his great mission — asking for inner capacity and outward ease.
insights.benefits: Especially powerful before important tasks, difficult conversations, or moments of anxiety.
insights.when: Before any important undertaking, before studying, before speaking publicly.
insights.explanation: Musa (AS) made this dua before facing Pharaoh. It teaches us to ask Allah for internal strength (expanded chest) before external ease.

translation: Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs.

insights.meaning: The statement of complete trust — declaring that Allah alone is enough and that His management of affairs is perfect.
insights.benefits: Ibrahim (AS) said this when thrown in the fire. The Prophet (SAW) said this in the Battle of Uhud. It is the ultimate declaration of tawakkul.
insights.when: In times of fear, uncertainty, when feeling overwhelmed, or when facing opposition.
insights.explanation: This phrase was uttered by both Prophet Ibrahim (AS) facing the fire, and by the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and his companions when warned of an army gathering against them. In both cases, it preceded a blessing from Allah.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَا سَهْلَ إِلَّا مَا جَعَلْتَهُ سَهْلًا، وَأَنْتَ تَجْعَلُ الْحَزْنَ إِذَا شِئْتَ سَهْلًا
transliteration: Allahumma la sahla illa ma ja'altahu sahlan, wa anta taj'alul-hazna idha shi'ta sahlan
translation: O Allah, there is no ease except in that which You have made easy, and You make the difficulty, if You wish, easy.

insights.meaning: Acknowledging that true ease only comes from Allah's decree and that He holds the power to remove any obstacle.
insights.benefits: Relieves anxiety when facing difficult exams, tasks, or seemingly impossible life hurdles.
insights.when: When facing something difficult, feeling stuck, or before a major test.
insights.explanation: A powerful reminder that obstacles are only as hard as Allah permits them to be, and He can remove them instantly with His will.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْبُخْلِ وَالْجُبْنِ، وَضَلَعِ الدَّيْنِ، وَغَلَبَةِ الرِّجَالِ
transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-hammi wal-hazan, wal-'ajzi wal-kasal, wal-bukhli wal-jubn, wa dala'id-dayni, wa ghalabatir-rijal
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from anxiety and sorrow, weakness and laziness, miserliness and cowardice, the burden of debts and from being overpowered by men.

insights.meaning: Seeking protection from eight critical things that paralyze human progress, mental health, and motivation.
insights.benefits: Acts as a cure for depression, removes the heavy burden of debt, and restores inner drive.
insights.when: Morning, evening, or whenever feeling overwhelmed by life's pressures.
insights.explanation: The Prophet (SAW) taught this specifically to a companion who was sitting in the mosque outside of prayer times, visibly overwhelmed by debt and deep anxiety.

translation: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up [when I was] small.

insights.meaning: A beautiful plea for divine mercy upon one's parents, mirroring the tender care they showed during your childhood.
insights.benefits: Fulfills the duty of a righteous child and brings immense blessings to both the parents and the child making the dua.
insights.when: In every prayer, especially in Sujood, or after obligatory prayers.
insights.explanation: Allah commands us to make this dua for our parents, acknowledging that we can never truly repay them, so we rely on the Most Merciful to show them His ultimate mercy.

translation: My Lord, increase me in knowledge.

insights.meaning: A direct, concise request for beneficial knowledge and deeper understanding.
insights.benefits: Opens the mind to grasp complex concepts, retain information, and apply wisdom in daily life.
insights.when: Before studying, attending a class, reading, or when trying to understand a difficult topic.
insights.explanation: This is the only thing Allah commanded the Prophet (SAW) to explicitly ask for an increase in, highlighting the supreme, unmatched value of knowledge in Islam.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ زَوَالِ نِعْمَتِكَ، وَتَحَوُّلِ عَافِيَتِكَ، وَفُجَاءَةِ نِقْمَتِكَ، وَجَمِيعِ سَخَطِكَ
transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min zawali ni'matik, wa tahawwuli 'afiyatik, wa fuja'ati niqmatik, wa jami'i sakhatik
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the withholding of Your favor, the decline of the good health You have given, the suddenness of Your vengeance and from all forms of Your wrath.

insights.meaning: Asking to maintain current blessings and health, while seeking protection from sudden, unexpected tragedies.
insights.benefits: Preserves one's livelihood, physical well-being, and spiritual standing against sudden trials.
insights.when: General supplication, highly recommended during times of ease and prosperity.
insights.explanation: A comprehensive dua to safeguard what one already has, recognizing with deep humility that all blessings and health are continuously on loan from Allah.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَصْلِحْ لِي دِينِيَ الَّذِي هُوَ عِصْمَةُ أَمْرِي، وَأَصْلِحْ لِي دُنْيَايَ الَّتِي فِيهَا مَعَاشِي، وَأَصْلِحْ لِي آخِرَتِي الَّتِي فِيهَا مَعَادِي
transliteration: Allahumma aslih li diniyal-ladhi huwa 'ismatu amri, wa aslih li dunyayal-lati fiha ma'ashi, wa aslih li akhiratil-lati fiha ma'adi
translation: O Allah, set right for me my religion which is the safeguard of my affairs. And set right for me the affairs of my world wherein is my living. And set right for me my Hereafter to which is my return.

insights.meaning: A holistic plea to rectify the three absolute domains of human existence: the spiritual, the worldly, and the eternal.
insights.benefits: Brings perfect harmony between working for this life and preparing for the next, preventing one from overshadowing the other.
insights.explanation: This dua beautifully balances the life of a believer, ensuring that success in the dunya does not compromise the akhirah, and vice-versa.
